metric_value: Over 130,000 units

metric_value: Number one volume leading provider of NAS solutions from workgroup to enterprise

metric_value: First Snap Servers launched by Meridian Data in 1998

metric_value: Snap Appliance (Meridian Data) acquired by storage giant Quantum in 2000
notes: Quantum Corporation acquired the Snap Appliance business

metric_value: Snap Appliance reborn as private company in 2002 after plans to go public were shelved
notes: Original IPO plans shelved; company spun out from Quantum as private entity

metric_value: Sells through manufacturers, distributors and resellers in North America, Europe and Asia Pacific
notes: Channel-only sales model covering three major global regions

metric_value: 6 named executives including CEO Eric Kelly

metric_value: APC, Computer Associates, Legato, Power Quest, SyncSort, Veritas, and Sun Microsystems

metric_value: Urgent need to keep up with storage requirements, discover low-cost alternatives, and have simple products

metric_value: SMB and enterprises with minimal or no IT staffing

metric_value: Inexpensive ATA disks in variety of redundancy configurations
notes: Use of ATA disks as cost-effective alternative to SCSI in NAS appliances

metric_value: Staging prior to tape backup; fixed content long-term storage; light/moderate-duty apps; data replication; PC client backup

metric_value: 1-50+ users per NAS unit with DataKeeper automated backup

metric_value: 320 GB to 1 TB total capacity with 4 ATA-100 disks

metric_value: 1.44 TB, 2.16 TB, and 3.0 TB across 12 disks in 3U chassis

metric_value: Up to 3 TB raw storage capacity with two Snap Disk 10 expansion arrays per 4500

metric_value: Proprietary SnapOS for workgroup; Linux-based GuardianOS for departmental/enterprise

metric_value: Snapshots, Windows ADS support, plus same ease of use as workgroup models

metric_value: Full NAS product range from desktop to enterprise covering all mid-line storage categories
notes: Comprehensive product portfolio spanning workgroup through enterprise segments

metric_value: NAS solutions positioned to address massive data growth experienced by companies of all sizes
notes: Implicit prediction that NAS demand would continue growing as data volumes increase

metric_value: Mid-line disk storage emerging as significant cost-saving opportunity
